titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Newman Scripts and Films
titlestmt.author: by Jennifer Meehan

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 9372975.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday March 28, 2018 at 21:07

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of scripts and film reels documenting aspects of the film and theater career of Paul Newman. Scripts include annotated typescript drafts of screenplays (annotations seem to be those of Newman) for films, including The Color of Money, that shed light on Newman's creative process as an actor and his contributions to the screenplay revision process, as well as bound typescript copies of screenplays with photographs that document the production of certain films, such as Cool Hand Luke. Also found are screenplays for films directed by Newman, including Rachel, Rachel, and scripts for plays in which he starred, including C. C. Pyle and the Bunion Derby. Film reels include recordings and outtakes related to the making of various motion pictures and the television program "The Battler," in which Newman starred.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from the Yale Drama Library, 2007.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Donated to the Yale Drama Library by Paul Newman, 1985-1991.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Containers 9-22 (film reels): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paul Newman Scripts and Films is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Newman Scripts and Films. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing, organization, and description, in 2010.

bioghist.head: Paul Newman (1925-2008)
bioghist.p: Paul Newman was an award-winning actor and director who starred in over fifty major motion picture films. He graduated from the Yale School of Drama in 1954.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of scripts and film reels documenting aspects of the film and theater career of Paul Newman. Scripts include annotated typescript drafts of screenplays(annotations seem to be those of Newman) for films, including The Color of Money , that shed light on Newman's creative process as an actor and his contributions to the screenplay revision process, as well as bound typescript copies of screenplays with photographs that document the production of certain films, such as Cool Hand Luke . Also found are screenplays for films directed by Newman, including Rachel, Rachel , and scripts for plays in which he starred, including C. C. Pyle and the Bunion Derby . Film reels include recordings and outtakes related to the making of various motion pictures and the television program "The Battler," in which Newman starred.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Scripts, 1958-1989. II. Film Reels, 1956-1981.

subject.part: Motion picture actors and actresses -- United States -- 20th century.
Motion picture producers and directors -- United States -- 20th century.

did.unittitle: C.C. Pyle and the Bunion Derby , typescript, annotated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes cast list and shooting schedule.

did.unittitle: The Color of Money by Walter Tevis, photocopy
"The Hustler: 8-Ball" by Darryl Ponicsan (Based on the Novel "The Color of Money" by Walter Tevis), bound typescript, annotated
Cool Hand Luke (1967), bound typescript with photographs
The Effect of Gamma Rays...on the Man-in-The-Moon Marigolds (1980)
Second draft, typescript, annotated, including notes
Fat Man and Little Boy (1989), typescript, annotated
Mr. and Mrs. Bridge (1990), typescript, annotated
"A Jest of God" (Rachel, Rachel) Workbook, Stewart Stern, For Paul

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of draft screenplay, shooting schedule, notes, expense reports, as well as some typescript and manuscript notes.

did.unittitle: "A Jest of God," final draft, typescript, annotated
"A Jest of God," final draft, photocopy
The Secret War of Harry Frigg (1968), bound typescript with photographs
Sometimes a Great Notion (1970), typescript, annotated
WUSA (1970) (formerly "A Hall of Mirrors"), typescript, annotated

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Containers 9-22 (film reels): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: The Making of Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid
